channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

Need

item.description:

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 244, Gipsy Road, West Norwood, Greater London

item.title: Dirt, 3rd July
item.description:
This stretch of the road never gets swept

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Ravensdale Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Outside 4 St Augustine's Road, 3rd July
item.description:
This e-bike/scooter company Voi has dumped overnight more than a 100(!) scooters/e-bikes outside 4 St Augustine's Road. There is not enough space in bike area for that amount of scooters. Most of them are literally in the middle of the road blocking one of the lanes. The drivers in the blocked lane need to drive in the opposite lane to bypass these stationary scooters. This is a major hazard as drivers coming into 4 St Augustine's over the Agar road bridge and Murray street have no visibility of drivers of this blockage and the resulting oncoming traffic! This is an accident waiting to happen. Yesterday within a timeframe of 2 hours I witnessed 3 near misses! I don't know who or this approved this within the Camden council but it is not logical to have this army of scooters on that area. Please talk to Voi and remove these ASAP. It is a major concern for us residents and drivers in the area.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Murray Street, Kentish Town, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Few bags with rubbish, 3rd July
item.description:
Rubbish has been there for a few weeks and not cleaned.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 20, Clarence Avenue, Clapham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Two chairs on pavement., 3rd July
item.description:
Can you collect the abandoned chairs on pavement
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Surrey Square, Old Kent Road, Greater London

item.title: Closed bins cause overflow, 3rd July
item.description:
Gascoyne One estate central bin area received two extra bins some time ago. The operatives continue to leave these closed and even locked causing a lot of overflow - there is not enough capacity with the four bins - we need the six

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Gascoyne Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Daily Dog Poo - Same dog and owner EVERYDAY, 3rd July
item.description:
A dog owner allows their dog to deficate on our pavement every day, right outside 2 homes with kids It happens everyday without fail, Neighbours are putting cameras up to take the matter into their own hands now as we have reported to the council mulitple times and nothing has been done
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Hervey Road, Kidbrooke
Report on FixMyStreet

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Salusbury Road, London

item.title: Around the garden beds/ verges/ border fence of the construction site around Premier Corner, 3rd July

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Salusbury Road, London

item.title: Bins in Sunray Garden are overflowing, 3rd July
item.description:
They’ve filled up quickly, mostly with dog waste, which is a public health hazard.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Red Post Hill, Dulwich Village, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Foxtail grass all round verges and spreading, 3rd July
item.description:
The grass has been cut in the larger areas but the edges have been ignored. This has allowed the foxtail grass to thrive and it is spreading quickly around the park. It is an invasive species and should be dealt with properly ie cutting short every time. It is dangerous to toddlers and dogs. The entrance opposite Clarence Rd has been neglected and is the worse I have seen for many years.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Parkside Avenue, Southborough, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tree problems, 3rd July
item.description:
Notices been placed, but nothing done.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Park Hill, Southborough, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
